response:
Short answer: because APIM and our Fleet flows expect a Rackspace X-Auth-Token, and reusing the existing Rackspace Identity flow was far simpler and more reliable than the Entra client‑credential approach.

Why we switched (summary):
- APIM authentication is built around Rackspace tokens (X-Auth-Token). APIM validates those tokens, classifies caller type/roles and forwards trusted headers (X-User-Type, X-Access-Level, X-DDI, etc.). An Entra app token did not integrate with that existing APIM validation/mapping.
- We already had working Rackspace Identity code and credentials in backend‑py (shared.janus_helpers.get_rackspace_identity_token and RACKSPACE_USERNAME/RACKSPACE_PASSWORD), so reusing it avoided new plumbing.
- The Entra client‑credential path required extra platform config (AUTH_TENANT_ID / AUTH_ALLOWED_AUDIENCES) and caused brittle failures in prod (JWT validation misconfiguration). That made the Entra approach more error‑prone to deploy.
- The backend running behind APIM couldn’t reliably rely on forwarded operator MSAL tokens, so the backend needed to present a token APIM actually accepts. The Rackspace identity token is the correct token for the targeted APIM /eventgrid/ensure endpoint.
- Overall this reduced complexity, matched existing RBAC/auth behavior, and fixed reliability problems seen when trying to use Entra client credentials.

Result: shared/fleet_event_grid_helpers.py now calls get_rackspace_identity_token(auth_mode='identity') and sends that Bearer token to APIM for the Event Grid ensure call.

"X-Auth-Token → used_by → azr-compliance-v3", "X-Auth-Token → contained_in → azr-compliance-v3", "X-Auth-Token → uses → backend-py", "X-Auth-Token → implements → ensure_bulk_operation_tracking", "X-Auth-Token → configured_with → governance registration client/config", "X-Auth-Token → implements → policy-engine", "X-Auth-Token → hosts → prod"], "recent": ["2026-04-09: azr-compliance-v3 Fleet Event Grid remediation token acquisition was revised again on 2026-04-09: instead of Entra client credentials, backend-py now reuses the existing Rackspace Identity flow backed by RACKSPACE_USERNAME and RACKSPACE_PASSWORD. shared/fleet_event_grid_helpers.py calls shared.janus_helpers.get_rackspace_identity_token(auth_mode='identity') and sends that token as Authorization: Bearer to APIM for the targeted Event Grid ensure endpoint.\n[specifics: service: azr-compliance-v3; component: backend-py switched auth from Entra client credentials to Rackspace Identity flow; env_var", "2026-04-08: On 2026-04-08 in azr-compliance-v3, implemented Event Grid inventory scoping and Rackspace ownership filtering. SyncEventGrid no longer scopes by customer_subscriptions + monitoring service blocks alone; it now scopes to managed monitoring subscriptions by joining customer_subscriptions to lighthouse_delegations, requiring managed_by_tenant_id to equal the Rackspace Lighthouse tenant and delegation_state in active/succeeded. The sync still uses the single Rackspace Lighthouse credential and intentionally does not acquire per-customer tenant tokens. Event Grid rows are now filtered before upser", "2026-04-09: azr-compliance-v3 Event Grid remediation was switched from a browser-side Fleet API call to a backend-mediated flow because Fleet requires application permission. The backend now acquires a ClientSecretCredential token for scope api://daf679b9-9134-42a1-9998-9cfac279747f/.default and calls https://apim.mscloud.rackspace.com/v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription, polling the returned statusUrl until completion. The frontend calls POST /api/event-grid/remediation/ensure instead of Fleet directly.\n[specifics: service: azr-compliance-v3 Event Grid remediat", "2026-04-09: On 2026-04-09 in azr-compliance-v3, integrated Event Grid remediation on the Event Grid Details page as a browser-side Fleet Platform call rather than a backend proxy because the browser reliably has the operator MSAL Bearer token while the backend, behind APIM, may not retain the raw Authorization credential in deployed environments. The page now uses metadata.coverage.customer_id (aliased from ddi), requests an optional Fleet-specific scope via VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_SCOPE, calls POST /v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription against VITE_FLEET_PLATFOR", "2026-04-02: On 2026-04-02 in /Users/benj8956/Documents/RackspaceClaw, added the first actual Entra-aware proxy implementation assets for the RackspaceClaw POC. Added runtime-config/proxy/docker-compose.proxy.yml, oauth2-proxy.cfg.template, default.conf.template, proxy.env.example, TLS cert placeholder docs, and docs/entra-proxy-poc.md. The proxy stack uses nginx in front of oauth2-proxy with provider=entra-id and passes X-Auth-Request-Email to the upstream, matching OpenClaw trusted-proxy guidance. Also updated scripts/serve_poc.py so the upstream honors trusted-proxy identity headers and exposes proxy co", "2026-03-23: Rackspace Identity token mint responses do not return x-tenant-id/DDI for operator/Racker accounts. The identity response is only a token mint/lookup payload; x-tenant-id must be supplied from platform context such as customer_subscriptions.ddi or another authoritative customer mapping, not inferred from the minted token response. For Fleet Platform operator API calls that use x-auth-token, do not expect the mint response to provide tenant context.", "2026-03-19: In azr-compliance-v3, X-Auth-Token authorization is defined in APIM, not OpenAPI. APIM validates the Rackspace token, classifies the caller as racker or customer, maps racker named-value groups to MSP-Admin/MSP-Engineer/MSP-Viewer, maps customer roles to admin/billing/observer, enforces route/category RBAC in api-policy.xml, then forwards trusted headers like X-User-Type, X-Auth-Method, X-Access-Level, X-User-Roles, and X-DDI to the backend. The actual racker group display-name values are environment-configured APIM named values, not stored in repo.", "2026-03-23: On 2026-03-23 in fleet-platform-app, targeted Event Grid remediation API failures in prod had two independent causes. First, azr-fleet-platform-prod was missing AUTH_TENANT_ID and AUTH_ALLOWED_AUDIENCES, causing backend function auth to return 'JWT validation is not configured; AUTH_TENANT_ID/AZURE_TENANT_ID missing' and 503 Authentication service unavailable for http_eventgrid_subscription_targeted. Fix: set AUTH_TENANT_ID=570057f4-73ef-41c8-bcbb-08db2fc15c2b and AUTH_ALLOWED_AUDIENCES=api://daf679b9-9134-42a1-9998-9cfac279747f,3eabce94-dd29-4358-b262-09a43f2ddb60 on azr-fleet-platform-prod a", "2026-03-25: On 2026-03-25, investigated 403s from Rackspace VPN IP 72.32.180.178 to fazure-oauth.mscloud.rackspace.com/register. Live AGW logs showed OPTIONS /register returned 200, while POST /register was blocked by global WAF policy waf-appgw-phoenix-prod-southcentralus-rax with rule 931130 (Possible Remote File Inclusion / Off-Domain Reference/Link) on client_uri=https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/inspector and redirect_uris=http://localhost:6274/oauth/callback/debug, followed by 949110 anomaly score block. Updated global WAF custom rules by moving BlockKnownPath from priority 5 to 6 and adding A", "2026-03-23: On 2026-03-23 in fleet-platform-app, Event Grid eligibility was tightened so Event Grid subscriptions are only created/maintained for monitoring-entitled service blocks. Changes were made locally in source_data, eventgrid activity/orchestrators, onboarding, validation, http_eventgrid, and OpenAPI docs. Customer-wide Event Grid orchestration now uses only Lighthouse subscriptions with computed_service_tier='advanced'; ensure_event_subscription_single now skips non-lighthouse or non-monitoring subscriptions; the targeted Event Grid API returns 409 for subscriptions without monitoring entitlement"]}
